thesis: Batch 5 is the deployment round: it keeps only validated structures, separates risk buckets explicitly, and refines around a narrow set of deployable candidates rather than continuing broad exploration.

benchmark.purpose: Primary passive benchmark for all campaign batches

universe.name: core_us_macro_v2
universe.selection_rule: Final deployment shortlist around validated GLD-defensive and legacy baseline structures

carry_forward_findings: Validated GLD-defensive structures have already separated into full-size leveraged, 0.75 leveraged, and non-leveraged control-grade buckets.
The final round should not reopen broad exploration.
QQQ/VOO/TLT remains only a baseline check.

candidates.hypothesis: VOO buy-and-hold remains necessary in the final deployment round.
GLD buy-and-hold remains necessary in the final deployment round.
QQQ buy-and-hold remains necessary in the final deployment round.
TQQQ buy-and-hold remains necessary in the final deployment round.
QQQ/VOO/GLD 126/7 is the primary non-leveraged control.
TQQQ/VOO/GLD 126/7 at full size is the raw-return ceiling control.
TQQQ/VOO/GLD 126/7 at 0.75 size is the deployable leveraged control.
QQQ/SPY/GLD 126/7 is the alternate non-leveraged control.
QQQ/XLK/GLD 126/14 is the tech-proxy non-leveraged control.
GLD 20/120 + 189d is the single-asset active GLD control.
QQQ/VOO/TLT 126/21 remains the final legacy baseline.
TQQQ 20/120 + 189d at 0.75 size is the leveraged single-asset control.
QQQ 40/180 + 252d remains the aggressive trend baseline.
